regime: Conflicted Transition (Stops Flip Long, Tape Still Heavy, Expanding Volatility)
summary: Three of four stops flipped long. The tape did not. MNQ closed -1.40% at 28,768.75 and its stop still turned long, because the stop trailed down 894 points overnight while price rallied back through it at midday. MGC and MCL both reversed one-session-old short flips, exactly the fragility yesterday's report flagged. Crude ran +3.73% on Hormuz supply disruption and is the only clean trend on the board. MES never flipped and closed -0.98% under its 7,540.81 stop, so the indexes disagree again.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band

setups.bias: Two-sided. Both outer bands tagged today and reverted; sigma contracted to 81.32.

setups.name: TSMOM-20
setups.bias: Borderline. MCL is the only clean leg; equity legs are whipsawing and disagree.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce
setups.bias: Borderline. AVWAP is overhead resistance again, but it points against the stop.

event.name: Housing Starts (June), Industrial Production (June), UMich Sentiment (July prelim)
event.consensus: Housing starts 1.310M SAAR; industrial production +0.2% MoM; UMich 51.0
event.actual: Housing starts 1.427M SAAR (+19% MoM); industrial production +0.1% MoM; UMich 54.4
event.surprise: MIXED, tilted positive. Housing starts beat big but internals were soft: single-family starts fell 0.2% to 895k (-3.2% YoY) and permits fell 3.0% to 1.367M. The 19% headline was multifamily, +76.3% to 513k off a 41% plunge. Industrial production missed. UMich beat by 3.4 points, up from 49.5, highest since February, with gasoline relief cited as the driver.
event.nqReaction: Two-sided. MNQ made its low at 28,408.25 at 13:00 UTC before the cash open, then rallied 638.75 points to a 29,047 RTH high around the UMich window. The 16:00 UTC bar closed 28,974.50 and crossed the stop, flipping it long. Price faded all afternoon to close 28,768.75, -1.40%, back under AVWAP.
event.crossAsset: The UMich beat was built on cheaper gasoline, and gasoline is what the Hormuz disruption is now repricing, so the survey's tailwind is already stale. Crude ran +3.73% as US-Iran strikes entered a sixth day and Strait traffic slowed. Gold reclaimed 4,000, +1.08%. Equities did not participate: the PHLX Semiconductor Index entered a bear market on a second day of AI capex selling and Netflix fell roughly 11%. Our measured MES -0.98% and MNQ -1.40% match the cash prints. Energy up, havens up, long-duration tech down is a risk-premium day driven by the conflict rather than the data.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: Split again with the polarity inverted from Jul 15. Then MES was long against MNQ short; now MNQ is long against MES short. Two breaks in three sessions is the read: the index complex has no agreement.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack. Context only.

observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack. Context only.

risk.ddProximity: Not binding. Book entered flat, full daily budget intact.
risk.sizing: Size reduced 50 to 75% per the DEFENSIVE band. MNQ ATR(14) rose to 143.96 from 126.28, so contract count comes down on the volatility change alone. Trend lines that must hold: MNQ 28,600.35 / MES 7,540.81 / MGC 3,981.97 / MCL 80.83.
risk.openRisk: Yesterday's correlated-short stack is gone. Three legs flipped long, MES did not, so the basket no longer nets to one bet. It also means no leg currently confirms another.
risk.note: Escalated from REDUCED. Two REDUCED triggers are active at once, which is the documented condition for DEFENSIVE: regime fit fell to BORDERLINE across three of four shown edges, and the volatility regime shifted (ATR +14% in a session, daily range 5.64x ATR). Two of four trend stops whipsawed inside one session. The binding constraint into Monday is weekend gap risk rather than drawdown: the US-Iran conflict is live, Hormuz flows are disrupted, and crude has moved roughly 13% on the week. No stop protects against a gap. Week ahead: Existing Home Sales, July flash PMIs, New Home Sales, claims for the week ended July 18, with Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM reporting.

psychology: Here is the trap today set. You spent two sessions getting short, the read worked, and then the stop flipped long on a red day. Every instinct says do something about it. Look at what happened: the stop fell 894 points overnight, price bounced 638.75 off the pre-open low, and they crossed at midday. That is arithmetic catching up with itself. MNQ still closed under its VWAP and handed back two-thirds of the bounce. Yesterday's report told you fresh flips are the least reliable state a stop has and to size accordingly, and today two of them reversed inside a single session. A stop is a risk tool. It tells you where you are wrong, and it was never built to tell you what comes next. When your system flips on a day price fell, trade smaller while the tape makes up its mind.

MCL81.7481.270.47UP13:5081.78Yes, 14:05

Read: All four broke up, the mirror image of yesterday. The MNQ opening range was 277.25 points wide, 1.93x its ATR(14), with the pre-open low at 28,408.25 as its floor. A range that wide again says the open had no agreement in it. MNQ was the only one to break up and stay out, and it still closed red, so the up-break was a recovery off a washed-out low rather than a bid with conviction.

Filters Monday: BIR not eligible (skip Mondays). HOF eligible, short only, 12:00 to 14:00 ET dead zone applies. MID eligible, 9:30 to 10:30 ET only. BNC eligible, no filter.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: MNQ: No long signal taken. Direction is CONFLICTED rather than a confirmed downtrend, so the filter no longer blocks E3v2 outright the way it did yesterday. Permission changed; the evidence did not. MNQ closed -1.40%, under AVWAP, with a stop flip one bar old and MES pointing the other way. This is the weakest kind of green light.

fullReport.html:

+1s28,925.25Yes
AVWAP28,843.93Yes
-1s28,762.61Yes
-2s28,681.29Yes, RTH low tagged -2.20s
-3s28,599.97No

Read: Both outer bands were tagged in one session and price closed at -0.92s, back near the mean. That is what the edge is built for. Yesterday sigma expanded to 96.32 and the bands chased a trend; today sigma contracted to 81.32 and price rotated between the extremes. The -3s at 28,599.97 sits 0.38 from the 28,600.35 ATR stop, so on Monday that shelf carries two meanings at once.

fullReport.html:
#Map row (IF)ResultMeasured
1MNQ stays below the 29,494.21 stop on hourly closes: short structure stands, 29,079 RTH low then the 29,000 psych and the 29,000.58 -3s are the downside referencesTRIGGERED - PLAYED OUTHeld on every hourly close; highest close 28,974.50. All three references reached: 29,079 taken out on the 00:00 UTC bar, 29,000 and the 29,000.58 -3s lost by 01:00. RTH low 28,665.00, overnight low 28,408.25.
2MNQ reclaims the 29,289.55 AVWAP and holds it on 2 hourly closes: first repair signal, the 29,509 RTH high and 29,532 OR high come back into scopeNOT TRIGGEREDNo hourly close above 29,289.55 at any point. Highest close 28,974.50, roughly 315 short.
3MES stays below the 7,613.08 flipped stop: the broad de-risk read holds, 7,548.25 RTH low then the 7,540.10 -3s are the referencesTRIGGERED - PLAYED OUTHeld on every hourly close, highest 7,552.00. Both references reached on the 01:00 UTC bar. RTH low 7,484.50, overnight low 7,473.00. MES closed -0.98% and never flipped.
4MGC 2 hourly closes back above 4,000: gold's break is rejected and the 4,025.50 stop comes into play as the trend testTRIGGERED - PLAYED OUTFired on the 13:00 and 14:00 UTC bars at 4,000.90 and 4,005.50, then held above 4,000. The break was rejected and the 4,025.50 stop came into play exactly as written: RTH high 4,029.00 traded through it, close landed 2.40 under at 4,023.10.
5MCL holds above the 78.56 RTH low on hourly closes while under the 80.59 stop: range risk between 78.56 and 80.59, with the 79.24 AVWAP as the midpoint tellTRIGGERED - FAILEDFirst clause held (lowest close 79.07); the second broke. MCL closed above 80.59 from the 11:00 UTC bar onward and ran to 82.52 (+3.73%), 1.93 above the row's upper bound. The range thesis was wrong and the stop flipped long.

Score: 5 rows, 4 triggered, 3 played out, 1 failed.  |  Month MTD: 39 triggered, 22 played out, 5 failed.
